block_05: based on the CySEC’s requirements. All traffic sources used to attract users from EEA countries have to be pre-approved and are regularly checked to ensure full compliance with the guidelines.
block_06: The recent assessment of your EEA traffic sources has shown that they do not meet all the requirements. This means that we will be forced to suspend your verification for EEA countries if no actions are taken in 14 days. Сonsequently, you will not be able to receive payments from the regulated balance. If you do not target the EEA countries at this time, let your manager know – verification for EEA countries will be suspended and your affiliate links will keep working in non-EEA countries as usual. If you do target EEA countries, please review your traffic sources for compliance with the regulator’s requirements and implement the necessary changes. If the list of traffic sources you previously stated as intended for the promotion in EEA countries has changed, please send the updated list to your manager for approval.
block_07: To help you understand what to look at first, we compiled a list of the most common issues:
block_08: Advertising binary options and digital options in EU/EEA countries.
block_09: If your materials are in a language that is widespread in Europe, such as English or Spanish, it should be clearly stated that IQ Option Europe Ltd. does not provide nor promote binary options to retail traders from Europe, and the following disclaimer must be present: “Binary options are not promoted or sold to retail EEA traders. If you are not a professional client, please leave this page”. In the case when the full disclaimer cannot be added due to limited space, use the short one instead: “Binary options are prohibited in EEA”.
block_10: Missing or not prominent risk warnings.
block_11: All promotions and invitations to open an account (registration links) must include a relevant risk warning that should be written in bold, in a text not smaller than the regular text of the material, in the language of the material, and in a place that would attract a reader’s eye to them. Risk warning texts can be found in the
block_13: Promoting to EEA audience instruments or features that are not available to them.
block_14: Besides binary options, some features, such as higher leverages, trading tournaments, and VIP status, are available to non-regulated traders only. Do not mislead regulated clients into believing that these instruments or features will be available to them.
block_15: Using misleading statements or focusing on the benefits of a financial product or a service only.
block_16: A balance between a potential benefit and the relevant risk has to be maintained. Claims like “trading is easy/exciting”, “suitable for everyone/beginners”, “start winning/making/earning money”, “trading leads to success, a change in lifestyle, financial freedom” or any other claims with similar promotional meaning must not be used.
block_17: Advertising IQ Option on a website that exclusively offers trading signals, social trading, copy-trading, auto-trading, robots, investment advice, or portfolio management services.
block_18: Traffic sources dedicated to such topics, as well as gaming or gambling, cannot be used for the promotion in EEA countries.
block_19: Using outdated content and promo materials.
block_20: Most of the materials published a few years ago no longer reflect the current trading conditions and have to be updated. As for the promo materials, please make sure to use the up-to-date ones available in the
block_22: Once you make appropriate amendments, send your manager a message with a list of the traffic sources you use for EEA countries. Please be advised that it may take a few days to review them.
